query: CHP olağanüstü kurultay çağrı heyeti Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi başvuru karar - latest status as of late June / early July 2026. Has Özgür Özel's team filed the application under Civil Code Art 75 requesting appointment of a 3-person çağrı heyeti to convene a CHP extraordinary congress? Has any court issued a decision?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the key finding:

## Summary

**Has the çağrı heyeti application been filed? — DISPUTED/UNCLEAR.** **Has any court ruled? — NO (as of July 1, 2026).**

### The core answer
The Özel faction **announced** it would petition a civil court on **Monday, June 29, 2026** to request a **çağrı heyeti** (call committee) after the Kılıçdaroğlu ("butlan") administration refused to process the delegate signatures for an extraordinary congress. But the sources contradict each other on whether it was actually filed:

- **Some outlets** (Sözcü, Haberler, Yeni Akit) reported June 29 that the team **was filing "today."**
- **But CHP Deputy Chair Gül Çiftçi told Odatv on June 29 that NO application had yet been made** ("henüz başvuru yapılmadı"), citing unresolved technical matters — the most authoritative single statement, coming from the faction itself.
- **Türkiye gazetesi** reported the filing was actually planned for **July 11, 2026** (the 45-day deadline date), with the congress targeted for **July 26**.

**No court decision** on a call committee has been reported.

### Key context
- Rooted in the **May 21, 2026 "mutlak butlan"** ruling (Ankara BAM 36th Civil Chamber) that removed Özel and reinstated Kılıçdaroğlu.
- **833 signatures** submitted June 17; 45-day rule invoked.
- Critical deadline emphasized by the faction: **July 26, 2026** (to preserve election eligibility). The **judicial recess starts July 20**, a complicating factor.
- Contingency: Özel's team reportedly obtained a shell party ("Anadolu Birliği Partisi") as a Plan B.

Note the terminology inconsistency in Turkish media — outlets variously named the venue **Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi** vs. **Asliye Hukuk Mahkemesi**, and earlier reports framed the request as a **"kayyum"** rather than a çağrı heyeti. Full structured findings with all sources and data points have been recorded.

sources.title: CHP'de gundem kurultay icin 'cagri heyeti'... Basvuru bugun denilmisti ama... Surec hangi asamada
sources.snippet: CHP Deputy Chair Gul Ciftci told Odatv that NO application had been made yet as of June 29; technical matters must be resolved first, but an application will definitely be made later if the congress is not held.

sources.title: CHP bugun mahkemelik oluyor
sources.snippet: Ozel's team is filing a lawsuit June 29, 2026 with the Ankara Civil Court of Peace requesting appointment of a 'call committee'; if approved, congress must be held within 45 days.

sources.title: CHP'de kurultay krizi buyuyor! Ozel'in ekibi bugun mahkemeye gidiyor
sources.snippet: Faction backed by 833 delegates petitioned the Ankara sulh hukuk mahkemesi on June 29 because leadership had not called a congress despite the May 21 mutlak butlan ruling.

sources.title: CHP adliye koridorlarina hapsoldu! Kurultay da ihraclar da mahkemelik olacak
sources.snippet: Ozel faction plans to file with the Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi on July 11 (the 45-day deadline), with congress scheduled for July 26.

sources.title: CHP'li vekilden 'Yeni partinin ismi ne olacak?' sorusuna yanit
sources.snippet: Suleyman Bulbul: on Monday June 29 the CHP will file a lawsuit in a civil court to request a 'call committee'; congress must be held within 45 days of petition; deadline July 26, 2026.

sources.title: Aciklama geldi: Yeni partinin ismi 'Yuruyus' mu olacak?
sources.snippet: Under Article 48 of the party charter mandating an extraordinary congress within 45 days of signature submission, Bulbul announced legal action on June 29, 2026 applying to a civil court for a call committee.

sources.title: Kurultay olmazsa yeni parti
sources.snippet: Ozel's team plans to file with the Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi to request a call committee; judicial holiday starting July 20 could interrupt proceedings; Plan B includes forming a new party.

sources.title: Kilicdaroglu harekete gecti! Belediye baskanlarina kotu haber
sources.snippet: Ozel faction led by Suleyman Bulbul plans to file a lawsuit with the Asliye Hukuk Mahkemesi to demand a call committee, following refusal to process the 833 delegate signatures.

sources.title: Ozgur Ozel'den karsi atak: Mahkemeden CHP'ye yeni kayyum istenecek
sources.snippet: Bulent Tezcan stated that if leadership refuses to call the congress, they will petition the court to appoint a new 'call committee'; faction gathered over 900 delegate signatures.

sources.title: Mutlak butlan sonrasi CHP kurultayi ceza davasi ertelendi
sources.snippet: 6th hearing of the criminal case on alleged 38th Congress irregularities held July 1, 2026; adjourned until September 16.

sources.title: CHP'de 26 il baskani daha gorevden alindi
sources.snippet: MYK dismissed 26 more provincial chairs on June 30 and referred 7 to expulsion.

sources.title: Ozgur Ozel yeni partiyi kurma tarihinde kararsiz
sources.snippet: Ozel's team acquired 'Anadolu Birligi Partisi' as a contingency vehicle; potential dates contingent on a pending court decision on the extraordinary congress request; judicial vacation July 20.

key_facts: Ankara Regional Court of Justice 36th Civil Chamber issued a 'mutlak butlan' (absolute nullity) ruling on May 21, 2026, removing Ozgur Ozel and reinstating Kemal Kilicdaroglu as CHP chairman.
The Ozel faction collected delegate signatures for an extraordinary congress; the most commonly cited figure is 833 (some reports say 839 or 'over 900' notarized signatures).
Ozgur Ozel submitted the 833 delegate signatures to CHP headquarters on June 17, 2026, with a 10-day deadline for the Kilicdaroglu administration to call the congress.
Party rules (Art. 48 of charter / Civil Code Art. 75 principles) require an extraordinary congress within 45 days of the petition; the Kilicdaroglu administration did not process the signatures.
CHP Aydin MP Suleyman Bulbul announced the faction would apply to a civil court on Monday June 29, 2026 requesting appointment of a 'cagri heyeti' (call committee).

Reporting on the venue is inconsistent: some outlets say Sulh Hukuk Mahkemesi (Civil Court of Peace), others say Asliye Hukuk Mahkemesi (Civil Court of First Instance); earlier reports referenced a 'kayyum' (trustee) request.
Multiple outlets (Sozcu, Haberler, Yeni Akit) reported on June 29 that the team was filing the petition 'today'.

CONTRADICTION: CHP Deputy Chair Gul Ciftci told Odatv on June 29 that NO application had yet been made ('henuz basvuru yapilmadi'), citing unresolved technical matters, and that a filing would be made later if no congress is called.
CONTRADICTION: Turkiye gazetesi (June 29) reported the lawsuit was planned for July 11, 2026 (the 45-day deadline), with the congress targeted for July 26, 2026.
As of July 1, 2026, there is NO report of any court having issued a decision on a cagri heyeti / call committee.
The faction's key operative deadline is July 26, 2026 to preserve election eligibility (avoid missing two consecutive congresses).
The judicial recess (adli tatil) begins July 20, 2026, which could interrupt legal proceedings.
If the court grants a cagri heyeti, the party would be legally required to hold the extraordinary congress within 45 days of the decision.
Contingency 'Plan B/Z': Ozel's team reportedly acquired a shell party ('Anadolu Birligi Partisi') as a fallback election vehicle.
On June 30, the MYK dismissed 26 more provincial chairs (bringing the total to ~36 since May 21) and referred 7 to expulsion.
On July 1, 2026 the 6th hearing of the criminal case on alleged 38th Congress vote-rigging was adjourned to September 16, 2026.

data_points.metric: Delegate signatures submitted for extraordinary congress (most cited)
Deadline given to Kilicdaroglu administration after signature submission
Statutory window to hold extraordinary congress after petition
Announced date to file court petition for call committee
Alternative reported filing date for court petition
Target date for holding the extraordinary congress
Provincial chairs dismissed by MYK on June 30
Total provincial chairs dismissed since May 21 mutlak butlan ruling
Next hearing date for 38th Congress vote-rigging criminal case
